metrics.name: Accounting correctness
metrics.desc:
Accounting model type,Pooled cToken market accounting
Deposits represented as,EIP-20 cTokens
Borrows represented as,Borrow balances tracked per account
Interest accrual mechanism,Market interest accrual through cToken exchange rate
Exchange-rate mechanism,cToken exchange rate increases relative to underlying asset
Reserve accounting model,Market-level reserves through cToken accounting
Fee accrual mechanism,Borrow interest split through reserve factor and supplier yield
Bad debt accounting,Market-level shortfall through undercollateralized account risk
Shared accounting logic across markets,Yes
Market accounting customizations,Interest model and risk parameters vary by market
Use math-sensitive logic,Yes
Upgradeable,Yes for Comptroller through Unitroller proxy
Known historical bugs,No unresolved core cToken solvency accounting bug found
Known historical reward-accounting bugs,Yes, COMP distribution bug in Proposal 62
Operation evidence at scale,Strong
Public documentation,Yes

metrics.name: Accounting correctness
metrics.desc:
Compound v3 uses a mature and well-documented cToken accounting model.
cToken exchange-rate accounting is simple at the user level but math-sensitive internally.
The architecture has operated at scale for years.
The Proposal 62 COMP distribution bug was serious, but it affected rewards rather than direct supplied-asset solvency.
Upgradeability and shared accounting logic keep residual protocol-level risk above the lowest category.

metrics.name: Liquidation reliability
metrics.desc:
Liquidation model type,Cross-collateral pooled lending liquidation
Liquidation trigger mechanism,Account liquidity below collateral requirement
Risk-management contract,Comptroller
Collateral valuation dependency,Price Oracle
Close factor,Governance-configured protocol parameter
Liquidation incentive,Governance-configured protocol parameter
Liquidation execution path,Repay borrower debt and seize collateral
Permissionless liquidation,Yes
Collateral seizure mechanism,cToken collateral seizure
Market-level collateral factors,Yes
Shared liquidation logic across markets,Yes
Dependence on external liquidators,Yes
Dependence on oracle freshness,Yes
Known liquidation bugs,Historical MoneyMarket liquidation vulnerability disclosed by Certora as pre-v3 / early Compound context
Historical stress evidence,Long production history without identified direct v3 user-deposit insolvency
Public documentation,Yes

metrics.name: Liquidation reliability
metrics.desc:
The Comptroller liquidation model is mature and central to Compound v3's design.
Liquidation logic is shared and documented, reducing idiosyncratic implementation variance.
The mechanism depends on external liquidators and correct oracle prices.
The pooled cross-collateral design has broader contagion potential than isolated-market designs.
No material unresolved v3 liquidation failure was found.

metrics.name: Oracle integrity
metrics.desc:
Oracle model type,Chainlink-based Compound v3 Price Feed
Oracle contract used by Comptroller,PriceOracle / Price Feed
Primary price source,Chainlink Price Feeds
Anchor or validation mechanism,Earlier Open Price Feed used Uniswap anchor; current docs describe direct Chainlink feed usage
Fixed-price support,Yes
Oracle configuration controller,Compound community multisig
Price feed update authority,Compound community multisig
Price scale / normalization,Prices scaled into Comptroller-expected format
Stale-price protections,Not clearly consolidated in current v3 docs
Fallback mechanisms,Fixed prices and governance / multisig configuration changes
Dependence on Chainlink,High
Dependence on multisig,High for feed configuration
Known oracle incidents,Proposal 117 oracle bug caused ETH markets to be paused
Public documentation,Yes
Audit / verification coverage,Open Price Feed audit and Certora verification history

metrics.name: Oracle integrity
metrics.desc:
Chainlink is a high-quality oracle dependency, but it is still a concentrated external dependency.
Oracle configuration is mutable through the community multisig.
Oracle errors can directly affect collateral valuation and liquidation correctness.
The Proposal 117 oracle bug is relevant protocol-level evidence.
Documentation is clear, but stale-price and fallback behavior are not fully consolidated in one public assurance artifact.

metrics.name: Governance and upgrade safety
metrics.desc:
Governance model,COMP token-holder governance
Governance module,Governor Bravo
Voting token,COMP
Proposal threshold,25,000 COMP
Quorum,400,000 votes
Voting period,3 days
Timelock delay,2 days
Timelock scope,Protocol parameter, logic, and contract changes
Upgradeable contracts,Yes
Admin-controlled parameters,Yes
Pause Guardian exists,Yes
Pause Guardian scope,Mint, borrow, transfer, and liquidate pause
Community multisig role,Pause Guardian and oracle configuration role
Known execution bugs,Proposal 62 COMP distribution bug; Proposal 117 oracle bug
Known governance attack / capture concerns,Proposal 289 governance controversy
Public governance documentation,Yes

metrics.name: Governance and upgrade safety
metrics.desc:
Compound governance is transparent, documented, and time-delayed.
Governance has broad power over protocol logic, parameters, markets, and risk controls.
Historical governance execution bugs show real operational risk.
Proposal 289 shows governance concentration / capture risk is not theoretical.
Timelock and Pause Guardian reduce risk, but do not eliminate governance failure as a protocol-level default path.

metrics.name: Upgradeability and admin control
metrics.desc:
Upgradeable core contracts,Yes
Upgrade mechanism,Timelock-controlled protocol changes
Timelock controls,Yes
Multisig controls,Yes
Configurable risk parameters,Yes
Configurable oracle parameters,Yes
Configurable market listings,Yes
Emergency pause controls,Yes
User exit preserved under pause,Redeem and repay cannot be paused by Pause Guardian
Admin blast radius,High
Upgrade review process,Public governance process

metrics.name: Upgradeability and admin control
metrics.desc:
Upgradeability is necessary for a live lending protocol, but it creates mutable-code risk.
Timelock improves safety by creating an opt-out window.
Multisig-controlled oracle configuration is a meaningful admin surface.
Pause Guardian design is narrower than many emergency roles because it cannot block redeem or repay.

metrics.name: Governance execution integrity
metrics.desc:
Proposal process documented,Yes
Proposal execution path,Governor Bravo to Timelock
Timelock queue required,Yes
Minimum delay,2 days
Review window,Yes
Proposal simulations,Used by security providers and community processes
Known proposal execution bugs,Yes
Ability to fix quickly,Limited by Timelock
Public proposal transparency,Yes
Execution tooling maturity,High but not flawless

metrics.name: Governance execution integrity
metrics.desc:
The governance execution path is transparent and mature.
The Timelock helps users and reviewers react before execution.
Historical proposal bugs show that governance execution is a real failure surface.
The Timelock also slows remediation when an unsafe proposal executes.

metrics.name: Governance power concentration
metrics.desc:
Voting token distribution,Concentrated enough to matter
Delegate concentration,Material
Quorum size,400,000 votes
Proposal threshold,25,000 COMP
Active voter participation,Variable
Whale / bloc influence evidence,Proposal 289 controversy
Multisig influence,Yes
Guardian influence,Yes
Mitigating review delays,Timelock
Public visibility,High

metrics.name: Governance power concentration
metrics.desc:
Token governance is open, but effective governance power can concentrate.
Proposal 289 is direct evidence that governance capture concerns are live.
Public visibility and Timelock reduce surprise risk.
Concentrated governance remains material because governance controls critical protocol surfaces.

metrics.name: Operational complexity
metrics.desc:
Core protocol components,cTokens, Comptroller, Unitroller, interest-rate models, Price Oracle
Market contracts,Multiple cToken markets
Risk-management components,Comptroller and market parameters
Oracle components,Chainlink feeds and Price Feed configuration
Governance components,COMP, Governor Bravo, Timelock
Multisig components,Community multisig
Liquidator dependency,Yes
External oracle dependency,Yes
Human intervention required,Yes
Cross-market dependency,Yes through Comptroller and shared collateral model
Operational actor count,High

metrics.name: Operational complexity
metrics.desc:
Compound v3 is simpler than many newer systems in accounting design.
It is still operationally complex because accounting, collateral, liquidation, oracle, governance, and multisig paths interact.
Cross-market collateralization increases dependency between markets.
Human review is needed for safe upgrades and parameter changes.

metrics.name: Privileged roles scope
metrics.desc:
Timelock role,Queues and executes passed governance actions
Governor role,Proposal and voting control
Community multisig role,Pause Guardian and oracle configuration
Pause Guardian role,Can pause selected functions
Oracle config role,Community multisig
Market listing role,Governance
Risk parameter role,Governance
Upgrade role,Governance / Timelock
Pause role,Pause Guardian
Unpause restrictions,Pause Guardian cannot unpause
Ability to affect user exits,Pause Guardian cannot pause redeem or repay
Role breadth,Broad but partially constrained

metrics.name: Privileged roles scope
metrics.desc:
Privileged roles can materially affect protocol safety.
The oracle multisig is especially relevant because pricing affects solvency.
The Pause Guardian is intentionally constrained and cannot block core exit actions.
Overall role scope is broad enough to increase protocol-level risk.

metrics.name: Audits
metrics.desc:
Compound v3 has unusually strong historical audit coverage.
Core, governance, oracle, and reward surfaces have been reviewed.
Audit coverage materially reduces unknown technical risk.
The main caveat is age: many v3 core audits are old relative to current protocol operation.

Verified liquidation properties,Yes / partial
Verified oracle properties,Yes
Verified governance properties,Partial
Continuous verification,Yes, described as CI-integrated
Public verification artifacts,Partial

metrics.name: Formal verification
metrics.desc:
Formal verification is a real Compound strength.
Certora's work covered important protocol properties and found issues.
CI-integrated verification improves upgrade safety.
It does not fully cover governance behavior, multisig execution, or all operational failure modes.

metrics.name: Testing
metrics.desc:
Testing culture appears strong.
Scenario testing and fuzzing are explicitly referenced.
Formal verification and tests complement each other.
Public coverage detail is not consolidated enough to justify a stronger reduction.

metrics.name: Monitoring
metrics.desc:
Compound is more observable than many DeFi protocols.
Governance and market states are public.
Security review processes are active.
Monitoring reduces detection risk but does not remove execution or governance risk.

Governance execution incidents,Proposal 62 and Proposal 117
Governance capture / attack incidents,Proposal 289 controversy
Reward distribution incidents,Yes
Pauses caused by incidents,Yes
Disclosure quality,Moderate to high
Resolution quality,Generally strong
Recency,Governance controversy in 2024

metrics.name: Incident history
metrics.desc:
No direct supplied-asset insolvency incident was found.
However, Compound has had real governance and oracle execution incidents.
The COMP reward bug was not a user-deposit loss, but it shows proposal-risk severity.
Proposal 289 keeps governance capture risk relevant.

metrics.name: Operational changes history
metrics.desc:
Compound v3 core architecture is mature.
Oracle, governance, and parameter surfaces have changed materially over time.
Changes are public and reviewed.
Prior change-induced bugs offset the benefit of mature process.

metrics.name: System maturity and production track record
metrics.desc:
Initial v3 deployment period,2019-era Compound v3 architecture
Time in production,Long
Usage at scale,High
Market cycles survived,Multiple
Architecture stability,High
Known unresolved critical issues,None found
Public documentation maturity,High
Security process maturity,High
Governance maturity,High but imperfect
Continued relevance,Yes, though Compound III is newer

metrics.name: System maturity and production track record
metrics.desc:
Compound v3 has one of the longest production histories in DeFi lending.
The cToken architecture has survived multiple market cycles.
Documentation, audits, verification, and governance infrastructure are mature.
Maturity reduces uncertainty, but does not eliminate governance or oracle risk.
